name: urql
description: A highly customizable and versatile GraphQL client with which you add on features like normalized caching as you grow.

capabilities.note: There is no built-in service-capabilities discovery/configuration in urql.

deprecated-objects.note: urql is not schema-aware and does not warn or typecheck deprecated object usage.

directives-on-directives.note: This is SDL syntax and does not apply to urql's executable operation handling.

executable-documents-descriptions.note: Since 4.0.0, urql relies on [`@0no-co/graphql.web`](https://github.com/0no-co/graphql.web), which supports executable definitions descriptions starting with 1.2.0 ([changelog](https://github.com/0no-co/graphql.web/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#120)). Older urql versions depend on the installed `graphql` peer version.

fragment-arguments.note: Not supported by urql's built-in parser.

on-error.note: The built-in fetch request body does not include the GraphQL `onError` request parameter.

one-of.note: urql does not perform schema validation or generate input object types; OneOf variables are sent as regular GraphQL variables.

semantic-introspection.note: Not applicable to urql's client runtime.

semantic-non-null.note: urql does not perform schema-based type generation or nullability analysis.

defer.note: Experimental multipart incremental delivery support for `@defer` and `@stream` was added in 2.3.0 ([pr](https://github.com/FormidableLabs/urql/pull/1854)). The protocol was later updated in 3.2.0 ([pr](https://github.com/urql-graphql/urql/pull/3007)) and 4.1.4 ([pr](https://github.com/urql-graphql/urql/pull/3389)).

stream.note: Experimental multipart incremental delivery support for `@defer` and `@stream` was added in 2.3.0 ([pr](https://github.com/FormidableLabs/urql/pull/1854)). The protocol was later updated in 3.2.0 ([pr](https://github.com/urql-graphql/urql/pull/3007)) and 4.1.4 ([pr](https://github.com/urql-graphql/urql/pull/3389)).

field-extensions.note: This is SDL syntax and does not apply to urql's executable operation handling.

http-batching.note: Explicitly unsupported by the built-in fetch implementation ([source](https://github.com/urql-graphql/urql/blob/main/packages/core/src/internal/fetchSource.ts#L21-L24)).
